cap_space_post_fa: TBD — Bills were over the cap pre-FA and required restructures/releases to achieve compliance; OTC shows approximately -$13.4M before all moves processed

key_departures.impact: Starting left guard departs on a 4-year, $61M deal to New Orleans. Edwards was one of the top IOL free agents on the market. Alec Anderson (1yr/$2.5M, versatile backup) is the primary internal replacement, creating a clear starter-level void at LG.

Eight-year Bills starter and All-Pro nickel corner traded to Raiders in a 6th/7th pick swap. Johnson was a cornerstone of Buffalo's three-CB defensive system. Scheme change to 3-4 under new DC Jim Leonhard accelerated the departure. Clear hole at slot/nickel CB.
Backup QB departed. Josh Allen has not missed a start since his rookie year; backup QB is low urgency but must be addressed for depth.
Starting fullback lost to a division rival. Gilliam was a valued blocker and special teams contributor. Loss is notable but FB is not a premium positional need.
Led the NFL with 5 forced fumbles in 2025. Departed as UFA. Void year triggered $7.2M in dead cap. With new 3-4 scheme under Leonhard, Bills did not re-sign him. Major hole at EDGE.
24 career sacks in six seasons, useful rotational EDGE. Voided contract triggered $2.8M dead cap. Unsigned. Depth loss at an already thin position.
Contract voided, triggering $11M in dead cap. Injury-prone but talented off-ball LB (12 games in 2025, 67 tackles, 3.5 sacks). Departure leaves a void at LB.
Veteran EDGE departing further deepens pass rush need.
Released for cap savings. Depth WR loss, minimal impact given WR need was already being addressed.
Released after failing a physical. Started in 2025 but was being cleared for cap purposes. Sam Franklin re-signing partially mitigates the loss at S.
Released for cap savings. Depth CB departure. Minimal direct need impact.
In-season addition in 2025, not re-signed. Older veteran depth piece, loss absorbed by DJ Moore trade.
Longtime Bills safety departed as UFA. At 35, primarily a veteran depth piece. Departure contributes to thin safety room.
Returned to Bills in 2025 after a year away, played solid football. Departed as UFA. With Johnson also gone, CB depth is critically thin.
Versatile DB/special teams contributor departed to Chicago.
Void year triggered $3.7M in dead cap. Veteran run-stuffing DT departed. Depth loss at IDL.

signings.trade_details: Bills sent 2026 2nd-round pick (No. 60) to Chicago; received Moore and 2026 5th-round pick (No. 164). Bills also guaranteed $15.5M of Moore's 2028 salary.

contract.notes: Moore's 2026 base salary ($23.5M) fully guaranteed. 2027 base salary ($23.5M) becomes fully guaranteed March 13. Bills added $15.5M guarantee on 2028 salary as part of trade. Contract runs through 2029.

signings.analysis: Top-of-market WR acquisition. Moore enters his age-29 season with four 1,000-yard seasons and familiarity with new HC Joe Brady from their Carolina days. The Bills' WR corps generated the ninth-fewest receiving yards in the NFL in 2025. Moore immediately becomes the WR1 ahead of Khalil Shakir and Keon Coleman. The $24.5M AAV and fully guaranteed 2026-27 salaries signal a multi-year commitment. WR need is fully resolved at starter level. Cost is a 2026 2nd-round pick (No. 60).

contract.notes: Re-signed before free agency to prevent him from hitting market. $13M AAV ranks 3rd among NFL centers per OverTheCap.

signings.analysis: Re-signing the Pro Bowl center locks up the most important piece of the Bills' interior OL through 2030. With 4 years/$32M guaranteed, this is a true investment — McGovern is a long-term starter. However, starting LG David Edwards departed to the Saints on a 4yr/$61M deal. Alec Anderson (1yr/$2.5M) is the only in-house LG option and profiles as a swing lineman, not a starter-quality solution. IOL is partially addressed: C is locked in, but LG remains a clear need heading into the draft.

contract.notes: 3-year extension restructured from previous $17.068M cap hit in 2026. Specific financial terms not yet reported. Extension lowers 2026 cap number while keeping Knox through 2028. Terms TBD.

signings.analysis: TE was not a pre-FA need. Knox (29 receptions, 417 yards, 4 TDs in 2025) is retained alongside Dalton Kincaid and Jackson Hawes, giving Buffalo one of the NFL's deeper TE rooms. The extension primarily serves cap management purposes, lowering Knox's bloated $17M cap figure while keeping a valued blocking and receiving TE in the fold.

contract.notes: Base contract is 3yr/$15.75M plus incentives up to $21M total. $10.12M guaranteed. 2026 compensation (signing bonus + base salary) fully guaranteed; 2027 base partially guaranteed with offset language. Effectively a 1-year commitment with a team-friendly out.

signings.analysis: Signed specifically as the Taron Johnson replacement at nickel/slot corner under new DC Jim Leonhard. Alford had a career year in 2025 (3 INTs, 13 PDs, 2 sacks in 16 games) and brings versatility to play inside, outside, and in the box. At $5.25M AAV with offset guarantees, this is a depth-tier deal despite his projected starter role. The contract is team-friendly and functions as a one-year prove-it deal. With Tre'Davious White, Taron Johnson, Cam Lewis, and Dane Jackson all gone, CB remains a significant need — Alford addresses the nickel specifically but the cupboard is thin outside of Christian Benford, Dorian Strong (neck), and Maxwell Hairston (ankle). CB need is partially addressed at nickel but still critical at the boundary.

contract.notes: 2yr/$4.1M base ($1.8M gtd), max value $6.1M with incentives. $300K signing bonus; $1.65M 2026 salary ($1.5M gtd); $1.95M 2027 salary. Up to $1M/yr in play-time/performance incentives.

signings.analysis: Directly replaces Mitch Trubisky as the backup QB. Allen is a close friend of Josh Allen, has prior Bills experience (2023), and gives Joe Brady a comfortable veteran in the room. Josh Allen has not missed a start since his rookie year, making backup QB a low-urgency need. This deal fully addresses the depth need at backup QB with a market-appropriate 2-year/$4.1M deal.

contract.notes: 3yr/$6.49M. Primary role is special teams; also provides safety depth.

signings.analysis: Franklin is a core special teams player and depth safety. At $2.16M AAV on a 3-year deal, this does not address the starting safety need left by Taylor Rapp's release and Jordan Poyer's departure. S remains a clear need for a starter-quality player.

contract.notes: 1-year deal. Terms not disclosed. Provides stability at punter after joining the team in Week 5 of 2025.

signings.analysis: Punter was not a need. Wishnowsky posted 41.8 net yards per punt in 2025, second-most in his career. Re-signing locks up special teams stability on a team-friendly 1-year deal.
Minimum-level deal to retain a rotational IDL. Does not address any starter need at DT. Provides depth behind Ed Oliver as the defense transitions to a 3-4 scheme.

contract.notes: 1yr/$2.5M. Versatile interior OL who can play guard or tackle.

signings.analysis: Anderson is the internal candidate to step into the LG hole left by David Edwards. However, at $2.5M on a 1-year deal, this is a backup/swing plan, not a committed starter solution. Bills retain Anderson to compete for the LG spot while drafting for depth. IOL remains a need.
Reserve/future deal for a third-string QB. No meaningful need impact.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: DJ Moore trade (4yr remaining, $23.5M fully guaranteed 2026-27) immediately installs a proven WR1 who has four 1,000-yard seasons. He pairs with Khalil Shakir in the slot and gives Josh Allen a reliable outside target. WR drops from critical to a depth/developmental need. A Day 3 WR pick to push Keon Coleman makes sense, but a premium WR pick is no longer warranted.

Connor McGovern's 4yr/$52M re-signing resolves the C position entirely. However, starting LG David Edwards departed to New Orleans on 4yr/$61M. Alec Anderson (1yr/$2.5M, swing lineman) is the only in-house LG option. LG starter need remains — IOL stays as a Tier 2 need but shifts from C-focused to LG-focused. Strong draft target in rounds 2-4.

EDGE was already a top need and has only gotten worse. Joey Bosa (5 forced fumbles in 2025) departed as UFA. A.J. Epenesa, Matt Judon, and Andre Jones Jr. are all unsigned. The team signed zero EDGEs in free agency. The defense is transitioning to a 3-4 under new DC Jim Leonhard, who needs OLBs/EDGE with 3-4 versatility. This is the Bills' biggest unaddressed need. Must address in Day 1 or Day 2 of the draft.

Matt Milano (12 games, 67 tackles, 3.5 sacks in 2025) voided his contract, triggering $11M dead cap, and is unsigned. Shaq Thompson and Baylon Spector are also unsigned. The team has made no LB additions whatsoever in free agency. The transition to a 3-4 scheme makes ILB/off-ball LB a critical priority — Leonhard's system requires thumping linebackers. This need escalates to Tier 1 alongside EDGE.

The Bills lost Taron Johnson (traded), Tre'Davious White (UFA), Dane Jackson (released), and Cam Lewis (UFA) in a single offseason. The CB room now consists of Christian Benford, Dorian Strong (neck), Maxwell Hairston (ankle), and newly signed Dee Alford at nickel. Alford's contract is team-friendly and functions as a one-year bridge — it does not solve the outside CB need. CB is now a Tier 1 need, particularly for a true boundary starter.

Taylor Rapp was released (failed physical) and Jordan Poyer departed as UFA. The only re-signed safety is Sam Franklin Jr. on a 3yr/$6.49M deal, primarily a special teamer. Cole Bishop (2024 2nd-round pick) is the presumptive starter but has limited NFL starting experience. S remains Tier 2 — need for a veteran starter or high-upside draft pick.

DaQuan Jones (rotational) voided and is unsigned. Larry Ogunjobi and Jordan Phillips are also unsigned. The roster currently has Ed Oliver and Phidarian Mathis as the only meaningful IDL options on minimum deals. The 3-4 transition typically demands bigger nose tackle types. IDL depth is thin enough that this escalates to a Tier 2 need in the middle rounds of the draft.

key_additions.headline: Bills trade 2026 2nd-round pick for Bills' new WR1 — four 1,000-yard seasons and built-in chemistry with HC Joe Brady
Pro Bowl center locked up through 2030 — 3rd-highest paid C in the NFL anchors Josh Allen's offensive line
Versatile nickel corner replaces Taron Johnson as Jim Leonhard's first free agent move — career year in 2025 (3 INTs, 13 PDs)
Knox stays in Buffalo through 2028 after restructure — red-zone weapon retained alongside Dalton Kincaid
Josh Allen's close friend returns as backup QB to replace Trubisky — been in this system before (2023)
Core special teams safety retained on a 3-year deal — provides depth as Bills navigate thin safety room

key_losses.headline: Starting left guard cashes in — Bills chose McGovern at C, leaving LG as the biggest remaining OL hole
Eight-year Bills nickel corner traded away as Jim Leonhard's scheme change reshapes the secondary
Led NFL with 5 forced fumbles in 2025 — departure leaves Bills with zero EDGEs under contract
Talented but injury-prone LB exits via void year — leaves Bills without a proven starter at linebacker
Returned to Bills in 2025, played solid football — departure further depletes a thin CB room
Starting fullback lost to a division rival — key blocker and special teams contributor departs
Backup QB reunites with Brian Daboll in Tennessee — replaced immediately by Kyle Allen

notes: All signings are confirmed/agreed upon as of March 11, 2026. Dawson Knox extension financial terms have not been publicly disclosed as of this update — total value and guarantees are listed as null pending reporting. DJ Moore contract reflects inherited deal from Chicago Bears (original 4yr/$110M extension); Bills added $15.5M guarantee on 2028 salary as part of trade. OTC shows BUF at approximately -$13.4M in 2026 cap space pre-FA; Bills were working to achieve cap compliance through restructures (Ed Oliver, Spencer Brown) and releases before the new league year. Post-FA cap position not yet fully calculable. The Bills made NO free agent signings at EDGE or LB — the two biggest schematic needs under new DC Jim Leonhard.
